make title description tag for my youtube short video on this topic
add strong seo keyword in title description and tag have #   जो छह चीजें जानता हूं तो भगवान की दो चीजें हैं।उत्पत्ति प्रलय से विश्व कब बनेगा और कब खत्म होगा? ये दो बातें जिसको पता है।दूसरी बात भूता नाम अग्नि गति किस काम को करने में आपको सफलता मिलेगी और असफल होगी।व्यक्ति विद्या अविद्या है जो सही और गलत व्यक्ति गया तो है।माला लेकर भगवान को प्रणाम करने के लिए लेकिन उसकी भावना सही है कि गलत।ये छह बातें हैं जो जानता है उत्पत्ति कहां प्रगति होगी, कहां दुर्गति होगी।व्यक्ति के हृदय में सद् भावना है कि असत् भावना।छह बातें जो जानता उसको भगवान कहते है।

Title: सृष्टि की उत्पत्ति और प्रलय के रहस्य | सफलता के मंत्र

Description:
इस शक्तिशाली YouTube Short में, हम जीवन के प्राचीन ज्ञान के छह मूल्यवान सूत्रों को उजागर करेंगे। जानिए सृष्टि की उत्पत्ति और प्रलय का सही समय किसी को कैसे पता चल सकता है, अग्नि गति के ज्ञान से सफलता और असफलता का अंतर कैसे करें, और सही व गलत विद्या की पहचान क्या है। साथ ही, सच्ची भक्ति और भावना की समझ को बढ़ाते हुए यह जानने का महत्व कि हमारे हृदय में सद्भावना है या असत्भावना। जो इन छह बातों को जानता है, उसे ही वास्तव में ज्ञानी माना जाता है। इस वीडियो को मिस न करें - सद्ज्ञान और आध्यात्मिक प्रगति की दिशा में आपका प्रथम कदम!

Tags:
#सृष्टिरहस्य #प्रलयकबहोगा #उत्पत्तिऔरप्रलय #सफलताकेमंत्र #अग्निगति #सत्यभक्ति #हृदयमेंसद्भाव #आध्यात्मिकज्ञान #भगवानकेसंकेत #आत्मग्यान #धार्मिकउपदेश #YouTubeShorts #भारतीयज्ञान #अविद्याऔरविद्या #भावनाऔरभक्ति #जीवनकासारज्ञान

Ensure that the keywords used are relevant to the video content, and consider what your target audience might search for when they are looking for content like the one you have created. Also, use hashtags sparingly and choose the ones that are most pertinent to the content of your video.

Title: Srishti ki Utpatti aur Pralay ke Rahasya | Safalta ke Mantra

Description:
Is shaktishaali YouTube Short mein, ham jeevan ke pracheen gyan ke cheh mulyavaan sutron ko ujagar karenge. Janiye srishti ki utpatti aur pralay ka sahi samay kisi ko kaise pata chal sakta hai, agni gati ke gyan se safalta aur asafalta ka antar kaise karein, aur sahi va galat vidya ki pehchaan kya hai. Saath hi, sacchi bhakti aur bhavna ki samajh ko badhate huye yah janne ka mahatva ki hamare hriday mein sadbhavna hai ya asadbhavna. Jo in cheh baton ko janta hai, use hi vastav mein gyani mana jata hai. Is video ko miss na karein - sadgyan aur adhyatmik pragati ki disha mein aapka pratham kadam!

Tags:
#SrishtiRahasya #PralayKabHoga #UtpattiAurPralay #SafaltaKeMantra #AgniGati #SatyaBhakti #HridayMeinSadbhav #AdhyatmikGyan #BhagwanKeSanket #AatmaGyan #DharmikUpdesh #YouTubeShorts #BharatiyaGyan #AvidyaAurVidya #BhavnaAurBhakti #JeevanKaSaarGyan

When using Hinglish in your titles, descriptions, and tags, it's key to consider the potential search habits of your audience, combining Hindi Romanized words with English keywords for optimal reach.

make it for this विधान नरक के बगैर कैसे हो सकता है? एक संविधान,एक रूल है रूल तोड़ो गे तो भुगतो गे। जेल जाओगे तो। वैसे अगर नेचर के रूल है,नेचर ने पशु को भी,एनिमल्स को भी उनको अधिकार दिया जीने का क्या कर आप जा जाकर उनको मार।अब जा जाकर उनकी पूंछ में पटाखे लगा रहे हो।आप उनके ऊपर कुछ भी ऐसा कर रहे हो जो उनके लिए हार्मफुल है।तो उसका फिर हिसाब कौन लेगा? आपने तो कह दिया पुलिस ने पकड़ा नहीं।आदमी यही तो सोचता है न कि पकड़ा नहीं गया। चलो बच गया पर बचा नहीं है।वहां तैयारी है ऑलरेडी वहां वह सब हमारे भागवत् में लिखा है।शास्त्र में लिखा है कि वहां जब मरने के बाद जो जिन पशुओं को तुमने खाया है,जिनकी टांग तुमने वह तोड़ तोड़ करके मजे लिए, वह तुम्हारी टांग तोड़ेंगे।तैयार रहना, क्योंकि तुम उससे बच नहीं, चिल्लाओ, पर बचाने वाला कोई नहीं होगा।सुनाओ गे। सुनने वाला कोई नहीं होगा। तो यह बहुत बड़ा कर्म का सिद्धांत है।जो समझ गया, वह कभी गलत काम नहीं करेगा।वह सबमें भगवान की भावना रखकर सबसे प्रेम करेगा।ऐसा नहीं कि जहां तहां जाए और मारते।

Title: Karma ka Siddhant: Narak aur Naitikta ka Mahatva | समझें कर्मों का फल

Description:
इस गहन YouTube Short में हम नैतिकता, कर्म सिद्धांत और नरक के अस्तित्व पर चर्चा करेंगे। क्या हमारे जीवन में किए गए कर्मों का हिसाब कोई रखता है? क्या नेचर के नियम तोड़ने पर हमें सजा मिलेगी? जानिए कैसे हमारे क्रिया-कलापों का सीधा संबंध नैतिकता और अदृश्य न्याय से है। अगर हम पशुओं के साथ क्रूरता करते हैं, तो क्या परिणाम हमें भुगतना पड़ सकता है? सद्गुणों को अपनाकर हम कैसे एक उचित और प्रेमपूर्ण जीवन जी सकते हैं, जिसमें हम प्रकृति और उसके प्राणियों के प्रति सम्मान दिखाते हैं। सही और गलत के बीच की रेखा को समझें और अपने कर्मों को दैविक प्रेम की भावना से संचालित करें। 

Tags:
#KarmaSidhhant #NarakAurNaitikta #NaitikJeevan #PashuKalyan #NatureKeRules #AdhyatmikGyan #DharmikShiksha #JeevanKeNiyam #KarmKaFal #DharmikLifeLessons #PrakritiAurHum #Adharm #SahiAurGalat #PraniyoKeAdhikar #BhagwanKiBhavna #KarmicPrinciples #SpiritualAwareness #EthicalLife #KarmaAndConsequences #MoralResponsibility

Remember to align the video keywords with the primary language of your target demographic and SEO best practices for better visibility.

Title: Karma Ke Siddhant: Kaise Bachenge Narak Se? | Understand Karma & Morality

Description:
Iss powerful YouTube Short mein hum baat karenge naitikta, karma ke siddhant aur narak ke asthitva par. Kya vichar karte hain aap narak ke bagair vidhan ke bare mein? Yadi hum prakriti ke niyam todte hain ya janwaron ke sath krur vyavhar karte hain, to kya iska koi parinam hoga? Is video mein explore karenge ki kaise hamare karmo ka seedha sambandh hai naitik vyavhar aur adrishya nyay ke sath. Hum kaise ek prem se bhare aur uchit jeevan jeene ke liye sadgun adopt kar sakte hain, jisme ham prakriti aur uske pranio ke prati samman show karte hain. Join kariye hume is charcha mein aur sikhiye ki kaise hume apne karmo ko divya prem ki bhavna se sanchalit karna chaiye.

Tags:
#KarmaKeSiddhant #NarakSeKaiseBachenge #SamjheKarmaAurNaitikta #PashuKalyan #PrakritiKeNiyam #AdhyatmikGyaan #DharmikShiksha #JeevanKeRules #KarmKaPhal #DharmikJeevan #PrakritiAurHum #Adharm #SahiAurGalat #PranioKeAdhikar #BhagwankiBhavna #KarmicPrinciple #SpiritualAwareness #EthicalLiving #KarmaAndConsequence #MoralDuty

These Hinglish tags and description help bridge the language gap and integrate cultural concepts, catering to audiences who are comfortable with both Hindi and English.

I will provide you with a transcript of a motivational YouTube shorts video as reference, I want you to write 5 different script similar to that.

Here’s the Reference:
A wealthy family volunteers at a community kitchen. After serving and dining with the less fortunate, they discuss their experience. The young daughter says, “Today, I saw real wealth. We have a chef; they cook for each other. We have a big house; they have a big heart. We seek entertainment; they find joy in helping others.” The parents are moved as she adds, “True wealth is in giving and sharing, not just having.”

Script 1:
The high school team decides to help out at a local homeless shelter. While working there, they meet people from all walks of life. As they hand out clothes and food, one of the teenagers, John, reflects aloud, "I always complained about outdated gear and not the latest phones, but these folks appreciate the smallest things. They smile at a warm meal, and they share stories instead of posts.” Another member nods, adding, “Yeah, real richness is in gratitude and community, not things.”

Script 2:
A group of corporate executives join a tree-planting event. After a day of digging and planting, they sit under a large tree for a break. One of the executives, Maria, sighs, "We chase bonuses, they nurture growth. We stress over deadlines; they savor the moment of life. They might not have bank wealth, but their legacy is everlasting green gold.” Her colleagues quietly agree, contemplating the oxygen-rich air, understanding that wealth is more than currency—it's the legacy we leave behind.

Script 3:
A renowned celebrity spends a day at a children's hospital. After playing games and performing for the kids, he chats with a young patient. The child says, "I may not have gone to premieres or walked red carpets, but these doctors and nurses are my stars. They work miracles with smiles." The celebrity, with tears in his eyes, admits to his followers, "Today, I've seen the most genuine stars. True wealth lies not in fame or applause, but in bringing hope and joy where it's needed most."

Script 4:
A family of city-dwellers spends a weekend on a farm, helping a family with the harvest. As they gather around a modest dinner, one city kid realizes, "Back home we might have the latest tech and trendy clothes, but this family has the touch of soil and the taste of fresh food. We’re disconnected; they're intimately tied to life's very source.” The family looks at their freshly-picked meal and understands that real wealth blooms from the earth and the care we put into nourishing it.

Script 5:
An artist joins a community mural project in an underserved neighborhood. Through the day, she collaborates with locals to bring color to their streets. Watching a diverse group find unity through art, she muses, "My studio is filled with completed canvases, yet these unfinished walls tell a richer story. We express through solitary creations; they create a shared masterpiece. Wealth, I see, is creating something that not only decorates but resonates with the spirit of community.”

Script 6:
A young businessman decides to take a break from his hectic schedule to coach a youth soccer team in his community. After their first victory, he sees the kids celebrating - not over scores, but over teamwork and mutual support. He reflects, “In my world, it’s all about competition and getting ahead. But here, it’s about lifting each other up. That’s a victory money can’t buy. True wealth is found in unity and joyous spirits, not just in winning or success.”

Script 7:
The staff of a tech startup decide to disconnect from their screens and connect with nature by joining a beach clean-up drive. As they clear the shoreline of debris, a shy intern comments, “We always talk about user engagement and platform growth, but look at this - the ocean and shore engaging, and our efforts helping them grow cleaner. We measure wealth by data and analytics, but maybe it’s really in preserving the world around us.”

Script 8:
On a busy weekend, a group of friends from a luxurious gym swap their workout for a day volunteering at an elderly home. After a day filled with conversations and shared stories, one of them, exhausted but fulfilled, says, “We push our bodies to be fit, but today I felt my heart getting stronger. In this place, it’s the warmth shared that keeps everyone going. True strength, and wealth, it seems, comes from the heart, not just from physical fitness."

Script 9:
A lawyer, used to defending high-profile cases, spends his afternoon at a legal aid clinic. After helping someone avoid eviction, he pauses to reflect on the heartfelt thanks he received and says, “In court, it's about winning arguments and big settlements. But today, it was about winning someone’s future back. The wealth we seek is often material, but what I found here today is a wealth of purpose and making a difference."

Script 10:
A successful author takes a break from book tours to tutor kids at the local library. As he helps a child finally understand a difficult concept, the child’s face lights up with understanding. The author thinks to himself, “I’ve always measured my worth by bestseller lists and royalties. But the joy in this child's eyes is priceless. Maybe the true measure of wealth isn’t found on paper or in accolades, but in knowledge shared and minds enlightened.”

Script 11:
A professional photographer decides to offer free family portraits in a low-income neighborhood. Watching families beam in front of her camera, she reflects, “I’ve captured so many high-end events, but the smiles here are the most genuine I've seen. We collect likes and followers; they cherish memories and togetherness. Wealth, I realize, is not in the prestige of my portfolio, but in capturing these unspoken stories of love and unity.”

Script 12:
An engineer who typically designs skyscrapers spends a Saturday building a playground in a rural community. As the children rush to play on the swings, he’s struck by a realization, “I build towering structures for a living, but this small playground reaches higher into the sky than any of my projects. We pride ourselves on achievements; they find pride in simple joys. Real wealth must be in the heights of happiness we help others reach, not in the physical heights we construct.”

Script 13:
A group of fashionistas swap their runway shows for a sewing class with underprivileged youth. They teach basic skills and together, upcycle old clothes into new designs. One of the designers says, "We always look for the next trend, but these kids look at how to give old fabric new life. They find wealth in resourcefulness and creativity. Perhaps the true fabric of wealth is woven not from threads of gold, but from the joy of remaking and renewing.”

Script 14:
A finance expert takes a day off to teach a financial literacy workshop at a local shelter. After the session, he shares with his colleagues, “I deal with big numbers all day, but today, teaching someone how to save and budget was the biggest deal. We value escalating portfolios; they value practical knowledge. Today taught me that wealth isn’t just in accumulation - it’s in empowerment and financial independence."

Script 15:
A group of gourmet chefs volunteer to cook at a soup kitchen, working side by side with the community. As they serve up plates, one chef remarks, “In our kitchens, we craft culinary masterpieces, but today, we made simple meals that mattered. We see wealth in exquisite tastes; they see it in nourishing fellowship. I’ve learned that the essence of wealth is not just in refining palates, but in filling stomachs and hearts together.”

i am using this as i am generating voice through AI so add proper punctuation pauses for this script  A group of gourmet chefs volunteer to cook at a soup kitchen, working side by side with the community. As they serve up plates, one chef remarks, “In our kitchens, we craft culinary masterpieces, but today, we made simple meals that mattered. We see wealth in exquisite tastes; they see it in nourishing fellowship. I’ve learned that the essence of wealth is not just in refining palates, but in filling stomachs and hearts together.”

Certainly! Here's the script with appropriate punctuation and pauses for an AI-generated voice:

A group of gourmet chefs volunteer to cook at a soup kitchen, working side by side with the community. As they serve up plates, one chef remarks:

“In our kitchens... we craft culinary masterpieces; but today... today we made simple meals—that mattered. 

We see wealth... in exquisite tastes; they see it... in nourishing fellowship. 

I’ve learned... that the essence of wealth... is not just in refining palates... but in filling stomachs... and hearts... together.”

Make sure to program the AI voice to take slight pauses at the ellipses and commas, and to convey reflective tones between the phrases for a natural and impactful delivery.

i am using this as i am generating voice through AI so add proper punctuation pauses for this script"A group of gourmet chefs volunteer to cook at a soup kitchen, working side by side with the community. As they serve up plates, one chef remarks, “In our kitchens, we craft culinary masterpieces, but today, we made simple meals that mattered. We see wealth in exquisite tastes; they see it in nourishing fellowship. I’ve learned that the essence of wealth is not just in refining palates, but in filling stomachs and hearts together.”

For AI voice generation, it is important to include clear punctuation to guide the AI on where to take pauses. Here's your script with added punctuation to guide the AI:

A group of gourmet chefs volunteer to cook at a soup kitchen, working side by side with the community. As they serve up plates, one chef remarks:

"In our kitchens, we craft culinary masterpieces... but today, we made simple meals that mattered. We see wealth in exquisite tastes; they see it... in nourishing fellowship. I’ve learned... that the essence of wealth... is not just in refining palates... but in filling stomachs... and hearts... together.”

The ellipses (...) indicate a short pause where the AI voice should slow down slightly, giving time for the message to resonate with listeners. The commas (,) represent shorter natural pauses within the sentence, while the periods (.) signify a full stop. This will help create a pacing that feels more natural and give space for the meaning behind the words to sink in.
